# InputElement

An element positioned inside an Input field, overlaid at the left or right side. Used for placing icons, buttons, or other interactive elements within the input.

## Import

```jsx
import { InputElement } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `placement` | `"left" \| "right"` | - | Which side of the input to position the element |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | - | The element content (icon, button, spinner, etc.) |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the underlying element |

## Usage

### Icon at the start

```jsx
import { LuSearch } from "react-icons/lu";

<Box position="relative">
  <InputElement placement="left">
    <LuSearch />
  </InputElement>
  <Input pl="10" placeholder="Search..." />
</Box>
```

### Button at the end

```jsx
import { LuX } from "react-icons/lu";

<Box position="relative">
  <Input pr="10" placeholder="Type here..." />
  <InputElement placement="right">
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Clear">
      <LuX />
    </IconButton>
  </InputElement>
</Box>
```

### With spinner

```jsx
<Box position="relative">
  <Input pr="10" placeholder="Loading..." />
  <InputElement placement="right">
    <Spinner size="sm" />
  </InputElement>
</Box>
```

## Features

- Overlays content inside the input at left or right positions
- Useful for search icons, clear buttons, loading spinners, and status indicators
- Positioned absolutely within a relative container
- Forwards refs to the underlying DOM element
- Can contain interactive elements like buttons
